In some he is a regular demon to be recruited, a sword bearing his name in reference to the novels the franchises are based on, and in others he is a central figure to the plot. * In the video game ''[[:en:Call of Duty: Black Ops II|Call of Duty: Black Ops II]]'', a fire-elemental staff known as "Kagutsuchi's Blood" can be obtained on the Zombies map "Origins". * In the mobile phone game ''[[Puzzle and Dragons]]'', developed by Gung-ho, Hino Kagutsuchi is a collectible fire monster. * In the card game ''[[Yu-Gi-Oh!]]'', alongside ''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'', ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and ''[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]]'', Kagutsuchi is one of the 4 Xyz Monsters of Bujin. * In the mobile game ''[[Monster Strike]]'', Kagutsuchi is a fire collectible, along with [[Susano'o]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Inari Ōkami|Inari]], [[Izanami]], [[Izanagi]] and more. * In the mobile game ''[[:en:Dragalia Lost|Dragalia Lost]]'', Kagutsuchi appears as a red Flame-type dragon with golden armor fused to his body, given the title of "Incandescent General". He is described as the rival of the game's similarly-dragonized version of [[Marishi-Ten]], with the two of them inadvertently creating a magical hot spring in the Hogo region of Hinomoto, the game's version of Japan, during one of their duels, when Marishiten caused Kagutsuchi's molten blood to seep into the spring's water, permanently heating it and leading the locals to dub it the Bloodspring or Twin-Wyrm Spring in honor of the dragons' eventual friendship. When Kagutsuchi is not waging war, he is usually basking in a hot spring or frying chicken, which he offers to his allies. *In the manga and anime ''[[Gintama]]'', the main weapon aboard the Altana Liberation Army's flag ship Amenotorifune, intended to destroy the Earth, is called "Hinokagutsuchi". * In the video game ''[[Ghost of Tsushima]]'', there is a Charm of Kagu-Tsuchi. This charm is granted by finding and honoring the Plum Blossom Shrine. * In the light novel ''[[High School DxD]]: Slash/Dog'' series, Kagu-tsuchi is the Shinto God of Fire and also the patron deity of the Himejima Clan whom in turned blessed them with the power of spiritual flames. A descendant of the Himejima, Tobio Ikuse is the wielder of the Ame no Ohabari, the divine sword which killed Kagu-tsuchi. The sword is infused with Kagu-tsuchi's divine flames, which grant it immense power to kill even a Demon Lord Gressil. * In the video game series ''[[The Alchemist Code]]'', the royal family of Wadatsumi, Rising Ashes, which consists of Mitsuha, Kagura, Zeke Crowley and Logi Crowley, uses a fire ability called Kagutsuchi, which takes the form of a phoenix when used. This technique is very powerful against a type of enemy, ogres. In difficult situations, Mitsuha, Kagura and Logi use an advanced form of the fire technique, called "Kagutsuchi Kiraboshi". * In the 2022 video game ''[[God of War Ragnarök]]'', it is implied that [[Týr]] reflects upon his knowledge of Kagu-Tsuchi as an inspiration for his combat tactics during his final encounter against [[Kratos (God of War)|Kratos]] in the game's free, and seemingly final downloadable content patch released in early December 2023. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Kōjin]] * [[Takemikazuchi]] * [[List of fire gods]] * [[Musubi]], part of the etymology of Homusubi ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== *{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} *Bock, Felicia G., trans. [https://archive.org/details/lccn_68-58914/page/n7/mode/2up.|''Engi-shiki: Procedures of the Engi Era'']. ASU Center for Asian Studies (Occasional Paper #17) ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85 Kagutsuchi], Encyclopedia of Shinto *Kagutsuchi on the [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kagutsuchi Japanese History Database]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Fire gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Killed deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Akiha faith]] }} [[sn:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[en:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[de:Kagutsuchi]] [[es:Kagutsuchi]] [[fa:کاگو-تسوچی]] [[fr:Kagutsuchi]] [[it:Kagutsuchi]] [[ja:カグツチ]] [[mni:ꯀꯒꯨ-ꯁꯨꯆꯤ]] [[pl:Kagutsuchi]] [[ru:Кагуцути]] [[sv:Kagutsuchi]] [[ta:ககுட்சூசி]] [[tr:Kagutsuchi]] [[uk:Кагу-цуті]] [[zh:迦具土]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]

{{Short description|Japanese fire god}} {{multiple issues|{{morefootnotes|date=January 2016}} {{refimprove|date=January 2016}}}} {{Infobox deity | type = Japanese | name = Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami | deity_of = ''[[Kami]]'' of [[List of fire gods|fire]] and the [[hearth]] | script_name = Japanese | script = 火之迦具土神 | other_names = Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto (軻遇突智命, 加具土命)
Homusubi (火産霊)
Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami (火之夜藝速男神)
Hi-no-Yakihayao-no-Mikoto-no-Kami (火焼速男命神)
Hi-no-Yakihiko-no-Kami (火焼彦神)
Ho-no-Yakezumi-no-Kami (火焼炭神)
| cult_center = [[:ja:火男火売神社|Honoo-Honome Shrine]], [[:ja:産田神社|Ubuta Shrine]], [[Hananoiwaya Shrine]], [[Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|Akiha Shrine]], and others | parents = [[Izanagi]] (father)
[[Izanami]] (mother) | offspring = [[Nesaku]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Kuraokami]] and others | gender = Male }} '''Kagutsuchi''' (カグツチ; [[Old Japanese]]: ''Kagututi''), also known as '''Hi-no-Kagutsuchi''' or '''Homusubi''' among other names, is the [[kami]] of [[:en:Fire worship|fire]] in classical [[Japanese mythology]].{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Kagutsuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85|access-date=2020-12-16|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} ==Mythology== Kagutsuchi's birth burned his mother [[Izanami]], causing her death. His father [[Izanagi]], in his grief, beheaded Kagutsuchi with his sword, [[Ame-no-ohabari|Ame no Ohabari]] (天之尾羽張), and cut his body into eight pieces, which became eight [[volcano]]es. Kagutsuchi's corpse created numerous deities, which typically includes [[Watatsumi]], [[Kuraokami]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Futsunushi]], [[:en:Amatsumikaboshi|Amatsumikaboshi]], and [[Ōyamatsumi]].{{cite web |last1=Cartwright |first1=Mark |title=Kagutsuchi |url=https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |website=World History Encyclopedia |publisher=World History Publishing |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20210412182013/https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |archive-date=12 April 2021 |date=27 June 2017 |url-status=live}} {{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Ōyamatsumi|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9706|access-date=2024-07-05|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} Kagutsuchi's birth, in [[Japanese mythology]], comes at the end of the creation of the world and marks the beginning of death.{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} In the ''[[Engishiki]]'', a source which contains the myth, Izanami, in her death throes, bears the water goddess [[Mizuhanome]], instructing her to pacify Kagu-tsuchi if he should become violent. This story also contains references to traditional fire-fighting tools: gourds for carrying water and wet clay and water reeds for smothering fires. ==Name== The name ''Kagutsuchi'' was originally a compound phrase, consisting of ''kagu'', an Old Japanese root verb meaning "to shine"; ''tsu'', the Old Japanese possessive particle; and ''chi'', an Old Japanese root meaning "force, power".{{nihongo|Kokugo Dai Jiten, Revised Edition|国語大辞典（新装版）}} (in Japanese), [[Tōkyō]]: [[Shogakukan]], 1988 ==Popular culture== {{In popular culture|section|date=March 2023}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Sailor Moon]]'', a character named [[Hino Rei]] uses flame magic. {{Dubious|date=March 2023|reason=Wikipedia's pages about the various Hikawa shrines indicate that Susanoo-no-Mikoto is enshrined in them, not Kagu-tsuchi.}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Kimetsu no Yaiba]]'', there is a dance called Hinokami Kagura (lit. Dance of the Fire God), referencing Kagu-tsuchi, that is later transformed into a Breathing Style by the protagonist, [[Tanjiro Kamado]]. * In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Fairy Tail (anime)|Fairy Tail]]'', a character named [[List of Fairy Tail characters#Grimoire Heart|Zancrow]] uses the Flame God Slayer Magic and has an attack named after Kagutsuchi. * In the manga ''Noragami'', Kagutsuchi appears during the subjugation of Bishamon and is shown to be capable of blowing fire. * In the anime ''[[Mai-HiME]]'', Mai's Child is based upon Kagutsuchi. * In the manga and anime ''[[Naruto: Shippuden]]'', one of the main characters, [[Sasuke Uchiha]], is shown to have the ability to manipulate the black flames of Amaterasu into various weapons. This is referred to as Blaze Release: Kagutsuchi, which refers to the fire god. * In the anime ''[[:en:Ao No Exorcist|Ao No Exorcist]]'', the hometown to several of the characters is Kyoto, where a powerful sword was made by Kagu-Tsuchi long ago. * In the video game series ''[[:en:BlazBlue|BlazBlue]]'', the main setting of the first two games is the 13th Hierarchical City of Kagutsuchi. ** In the same video game series, one of the playable characters, Nine the Phantom, can summon Hi no Kagutsuchi, an enormous fire elemental. * In the video game series ''[[:en:Brave Frontier|Brave Frontier]]'', Kagatsuchi is a fire-based battle unit. He takes the form of a centaur, and is described as "a disastrous beast". * In the manga ''[[Rurouni Kenshin]]'', the main antagonist of the Kyoto Arc, [[Shishio Makoto]], uses a technique called "The Final Secret Sword: Kagutsuchi" as a last resort "trump card", bringing down a cyclone of flames that his sword can produce at his enemy. * In the ''[[Shin Megami Tensei]]'' and ''[[Persona (series)|Persona]]'' franchises, Kagutsuchi is a recurring figure in various titles. In some he is a regular demon to be recruited, a sword bearing his name in reference to the novels the franchises are based on, and in others he is a central figure to the plot. * In the video game ''[[:en:Call of Duty: Black Ops II|Call of Duty: Black Ops II]]'', a fire-elemental staff known as "Kagutsuchi's Blood" can be obtained on the Zombies map "Origins". * In the mobile phone game ''[[Puzzle and Dragons]]'', developed by Gung-ho, Hino Kagutsuchi is a collectible fire monster. * In the card game ''[[Yu-Gi-Oh!]]'', alongside ''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'', ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and ''[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]]'', Kagutsuchi is one of the 4 Xyz Monsters of Bujin. * In the mobile game ''[[Monster Strike]]'', Kagutsuchi is a fire collectible, along with [[Susano'o]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Inari Ōkami|Inari]], [[Izanami]], [[Izanagi]] and more. * In the mobile game ''[[:en:Dragalia Lost|Dragalia Lost]]'', Kagutsuchi appears as a red Flame-type dragon with golden armor fused to his body, given the title of "Incandescent General". He is described as the rival of the game's similarly-dragonized version of [[Marishi-Ten]], with the two of them inadvertently creating a magical hot spring in the Hogo region of Hinomoto, the game's version of Japan, during one of their duels, when Marishiten caused Kagutsuchi's molten blood to seep into the spring's water, permanently heating it and leading the locals to dub it the Bloodspring or Twin-Wyrm Spring in honor of the dragons' eventual friendship. When Kagutsuchi is not waging war, he is usually basking in a hot spring or frying chicken, which he offers to his allies. *In the manga and anime ''[[Gintama]]'', the main weapon aboard the Altana Liberation Army's flag ship Amenotorifune, intended to destroy the Earth, is called "Hinokagutsuchi". * In the video game ''[[Ghost of Tsushima]]'', there is a Charm of Kagu-Tsuchi. This charm is granted by finding and honoring the Plum Blossom Shrine. * In the light novel ''[[High School DxD]]: Slash/Dog'' series, Kagu-tsuchi is the Shinto God of Fire and also the patron deity of the Himejima Clan whom in turned blessed them with the power of spiritual flames. A descendant of the Himejima, Tobio Ikuse is the wielder of the Ame no Ohabari, the divine sword which killed Kagu-tsuchi. The sword is infused with Kagu-tsuchi's divine flames, which grant it immense power to kill even a Demon Lord Gressil. * In the video game series ''[[The Alchemist Code]]'', the royal family of Wadatsumi, Rising Ashes, which consists of Mitsuha, Kagura, Zeke Crowley and Logi Crowley, uses a fire ability called Kagutsuchi, which takes the form of a phoenix when used. This technique is very powerful against a type of enemy, ogres. In difficult situations, Mitsuha, Kagura and Logi use an advanced form of the fire technique, called "Kagutsuchi Kiraboshi". * In the 2022 video game ''[[God of War Ragnarök]]'', it is implied that [[Týr]] reflects upon his knowledge of Kagu-Tsuchi as an inspiration for his combat tactics during his final encounter against [[Kratos (God of War)|Kratos]] in the game's free, and seemingly final downloadable content patch released in early December 2023. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Kōjin]] * [[Takemikazuchi]] * [[List of fire gods]] * [[Musubi]], part of the etymology of Homusubi ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== *{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} *Bock, Felicia G., trans. [https://archive.org/details/lccn_68-58914/page/n7/mode/2up.|''Engi-shiki: Procedures of the Engi Era'']. ASU Center for Asian Studies (Occasional Paper #17) ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85 Kagutsuchi], Encyclopedia of Shinto *Kagutsuchi on the [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kagutsuchi Japanese History Database]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Fire gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Killed deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Akiha faith]] }} [[sn:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[en:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[de:Kagutsuchi]] [[es:Kagutsuchi]] [[fa:کاگو-تسوچی]] [[fr:Kagutsuchi]] [[it:Kagutsuchi]] [[ja:カグツチ]] [[mni:ꯀꯒꯨ-ꯁꯨꯆꯤ]] [[pl:Kagutsuchi]] [[ru:Кагуцути]] [[sv:Kagutsuchi]] [[ta:ககுட்சூசி]] [[tr:Kagutsuchi]] [[uk:Кагу-цуті]] [[zh:迦具土]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]

In difficult situations, Mitsuha, Kagura and Logi use an advanced form of the fire technique, called "Kagutsuchi Kiraboshi". * In the 2022 video game ''[[God of War Ragnarök]]'', it is implied that [[Týr]] reflects upon his knowledge of Kagu-Tsuchi as an inspiration for his combat tactics during his final encounter against [[Kratos (God of War)|Kratos]] in the game's free, and seemingly final downloadable content patch released in early December 2023. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Kōjin]] * [[Takemikazuchi]] * [[List of fire gods]] * [[Musubi]], part of the etymology of Homusubi ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== *{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} *Bock, Felicia G., trans. [https://archive.org/details/lccn_68-58914/page/n7/mode/2up.|''Engi-shiki: Procedures of the Engi Era'']. ASU Center for Asian Studies (Occasional Paper #17) ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85 Kagutsuchi], Encyclopedia of Shinto *Kagutsuchi on the [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kagutsuchi Japanese History Database]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Fire gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Killed deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Akiha faith]] }} [[sn:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[en:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[de:Kagutsuchi]] [[es:Kagutsuchi]] [[fa:کاگو-تسوچی]] [[fr:Kagutsuchi]] [[it:Kagutsuchi]] [[ja:カグツチ]] [[mni:ꯀꯒꯨ-ꯁꯨꯆꯤ]] [[pl:Kagutsuchi]] [[ru:Кагуцути]] [[sv:Kagutsuchi]] [[ta:ககுட்சூசி]] [[tr:Kagutsuchi]] [[uk:Кагу-цуті]] [[zh:迦具土]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]

{{Short description|Japanese fire god}} {{multiple issues|{{morefootnotes|date=January 2016}} {{refimprove|date=January 2016}}}} {{Infobox deity | type = Japanese | name = Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami | deity_of = ''[[Kami]]'' of [[List of fire gods|fire]] and the [[hearth]] | script_name = Japanese | script = 火之迦具土神 | other_names = Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto (軻遇突智命, 加具土命)
Homusubi (火産霊)
Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami (火之夜藝速男神)
Hi-no-Yakihayao-no-Mikoto-no-Kami (火焼速男命神)
Hi-no-Yakihiko-no-Kami (火焼彦神)
Ho-no-Yakezumi-no-Kami (火焼炭神)
| cult_center = [[:ja:火男火売神社|Honoo-Honome Shrine]], [[:ja:産田神社|Ubuta Shrine]], [[Hananoiwaya Shrine]], [[Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|Akiha Shrine]], and others | parents = [[Izanagi]] (father)
[[Izanami]] (mother) | offspring = [[Nesaku]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Kuraokami]] and others | gender = Male }} '''Kagutsuchi''' (カグツチ; [[Old Japanese]]: ''Kagututi''), also known as '''Hi-no-Kagutsuchi''' or '''Homusubi''' among other names, is the [[kami]] of [[:en:Fire worship|fire]] in classical [[Japanese mythology]].{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Kagutsuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85|access-date=2020-12-16|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} ==Mythology== Kagutsuchi's birth burned his mother [[Izanami]], causing her death. His father [[Izanagi]], in his grief, beheaded Kagutsuchi with his sword, [[Ame-no-ohabari|Ame no Ohabari]] (天之尾羽張), and cut his body into eight pieces, which became eight [[volcano]]es. Kagutsuchi's corpse created numerous deities, which typically includes [[Watatsumi]], [[Kuraokami]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Futsunushi]], [[:en:Amatsumikaboshi|Amatsumikaboshi]], and [[Ōyamatsumi]].{{cite web |last1=Cartwright |first1=Mark |title=Kagutsuchi |url=https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |website=World History Encyclopedia |publisher=World History Publishing |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20210412182013/https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |archive-date=12 April 2021 |date=27 June 2017 |url-status=live}} {{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Ōyamatsumi|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9706|access-date=2024-07-05|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} Kagutsuchi's birth, in [[Japanese mythology]], comes at the end of the creation of the world and marks the beginning of death.{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} In the ''[[Engishiki]]'', a source which contains the myth, Izanami, in her death throes, bears the water goddess [[Mizuhanome]], instructing her to pacify Kagu-tsuchi if he should become violent. This story also contains references to traditional fire-fighting tools: gourds for carrying water and wet clay and water reeds for smothering fires. ==Name== The name ''Kagutsuchi'' was originally a compound phrase, consisting of ''kagu'', an Old Japanese root verb meaning "to shine"; ''tsu'', the Old Japanese possessive particle; and ''chi'', an Old Japanese root meaning "force, power".{{nihongo|Kokugo Dai Jiten, Revised Edition|国語大辞典（新装版）}} (in Japanese), [[Tōkyō]]: [[Shogakukan]], 1988 ==Popular culture== {{In popular culture|section|date=March 2023}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Sailor Moon]]'', a character named [[Hino Rei]] uses flame magic. {{Dubious|date=March 2023|reason=Wikipedia's pages about the various Hikawa shrines indicate that Susanoo-no-Mikoto is enshrined in them, not Kagu-tsuchi.}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Kimetsu no Yaiba]]'', there is a dance called Hinokami Kagura (lit. Dance of the Fire God), referencing Kagu-tsuchi, that is later transformed into a Breathing Style by the protagonist, [[Tanjiro Kamado]]. * In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Fairy Tail (anime)|Fairy Tail]]'', a character named [[List of Fairy Tail characters#Grimoire Heart|Zancrow]] uses the Flame God Slayer Magic and has an attack named after Kagutsuchi. * In the manga ''Noragami'', Kagutsuchi appears during the subjugation of Bishamon and is shown to be capable of blowing fire. * In the anime ''[[Mai-HiME]]'', Mai's Child is based upon Kagutsuchi. * In the manga and anime ''[[Naruto: Shippuden]]'', one of the main characters, [[Sasuke Uchiha]], is shown to have the ability to manipulate the black flames of Amaterasu into various weapons. This is referred to as Blaze Release: Kagutsuchi, which refers to the fire god. * In the anime ''[[:en:Ao No Exorcist|Ao No Exorcist]]'', the hometown to several of the characters is Kyoto, where a powerful sword was made by Kagu-Tsuchi long ago. * In the video game series ''[[:en:BlazBlue|BlazBlue]]'', the main setting of the first two games is the 13th Hierarchical City of Kagutsuchi. ** In the same video game series, one of the playable characters, Nine the Phantom, can summon Hi no Kagutsuchi, an enormous fire elemental. * In the video game series ''[[:en:Brave Frontier|Brave Frontier]]'', Kagatsuchi is a fire-based battle unit. He takes the form of a centaur, and is described as "a disastrous beast". * In the manga ''[[Rurouni Kenshin]]'', the main antagonist of the Kyoto Arc, [[Shishio Makoto]], uses a technique called "The Final Secret Sword: Kagutsuchi" as a last resort "trump card", bringing down a cyclone of flames that his sword can produce at his enemy. * In the ''[[Shin Megami Tensei]]'' and ''[[Persona (series)|Persona]]'' franchises, Kagutsuchi is a recurring figure in various titles. In some he is a regular demon to be recruited, a sword bearing his name in reference to the novels the franchises are based on, and in others he is a central figure to the plot. * In the video game ''[[:en:Call of Duty: Black Ops II|Call of Duty: Black Ops II]]'', a fire-elemental staff known as "Kagutsuchi's Blood" can be obtained on the Zombies map "Origins". * In the mobile phone game ''[[Puzzle and Dragons]]'', developed by Gung-ho, Hino Kagutsuchi is a collectible fire monster. * In the card game ''[[Yu-Gi-Oh!]]'', alongside ''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'', ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and ''[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]]'', Kagutsuchi is one of the 4 Xyz Monsters of Bujin. * In the mobile game ''[[Monster Strike]]'', Kagutsuchi is a fire collectible, along with [[Susano'o]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Inari Ōkami|Inari]], [[Izanami]], [[Izanagi]] and more. * In the mobile game ''[[:en:Dragalia Lost|Dragalia Lost]]'', Kagutsuchi appears as a red Flame-type dragon with golden armor fused to his body, given the title of "Incandescent General". He is described as the rival of the game's similarly-dragonized version of [[Marishi-Ten]], with the two of them inadvertently creating a magical hot spring in the Hogo region of Hinomoto, the game's version of Japan, during one of their duels, when Marishiten caused Kagutsuchi's molten blood to seep into the spring's water, permanently heating it and leading the locals to dub it the Bloodspring or Twin-Wyrm Spring in honor of the dragons' eventual friendship. When Kagutsuchi is not waging war, he is usually basking in a hot spring or frying chicken, which he offers to his allies. *In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Gintama|Gintama]]'', the main weapon aboard the Altana Liberation Army's flag ship Amenotorifune, intended to destroy the Earth, is called "Hinokagutsuchi". * In the video game ''[[:en:Ghost of Tsushima|Ghost of Tsushima]]'', there is a Charm of Kagu-Tsuchi. This charm is granted by finding and honoring the Plum Blossom Shrine. * In the light novel ''[[High School DxD]]: Slash/Dog'' series, Kagu-tsuchi is the Shinto God of Fire and also the patron deity of the Himejima Clan whom in turned blessed them with the power of spiritual flames. A descendant of the Himejima, Tobio Ikuse is the wielder of the Ame no Ohabari, the divine sword which killed Kagu-tsuchi. The sword is infused with Kagu-tsuchi's divine flames, which grant it immense power to kill even a Demon Lord Gressil. * In the video game series ''[[The Alchemist Code]]'', the royal family of Wadatsumi, Rising Ashes, which consists of Mitsuha, Kagura, Zeke Crowley and Logi Crowley, uses a fire ability called Kagutsuchi, which takes the form of a phoenix when used. This technique is very powerful against a type of enemy, ogres. In difficult situations, Mitsuha, Kagura and Logi use an advanced form of the fire technique, called "Kagutsuchi Kiraboshi". * In the 2022 video game ''[[God of War Ragnarök]]'', it is implied that [[Týr]] reflects upon his knowledge of Kagu-Tsuchi as an inspiration for his combat tactics during his final encounter against [[Kratos (God of War)|Kratos]] in the game's free, and seemingly final downloadable content patch released in early December 2023. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Kōjin]] * [[Takemikazuchi]] * [[List of fire gods]] * [[Musubi]], part of the etymology of Homusubi ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== *{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} *Bock, Felicia G., trans. [https://archive.org/details/lccn_68-58914/page/n7/mode/2up.|''Engi-shiki: Procedures of the Engi Era'']. ASU Center for Asian Studies (Occasional Paper #17) ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85 Kagutsuchi], Encyclopedia of Shinto *Kagutsuchi on the [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kagutsuchi Japanese History Database]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Fire gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Killed deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Akiha faith]] }} [[sn:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[en:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[de:Kagutsuchi]] [[es:Kagutsuchi]] [[fa:کاگو-تسوچی]] [[fr:Kagutsuchi]] [[it:Kagutsuchi]] [[ja:カグツチ]] [[mni:ꯀꯒꯨ-ꯁꯨꯆꯤ]] [[pl:Kagutsuchi]] [[ru:Кагуцути]] [[sv:Kagutsuchi]] [[ta:ககுட்சூசி]] [[tr:Kagutsuchi]] [[uk:Кагу-цуті]] [[zh:迦具土]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]

| cult_center = [[:ja:火男火売神社|Honoo-Honome Shrine]], [[:ja:産田神社|Ubuta Shrine]], [[:en:Hananoiwaya Shrine|Hananoiwaya Shrine]], [[Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|Akiha Shrine]], and others | parents = [[Izanagi]] (father)
[[Izanami]] (mother) | offspring = [[Nesaku]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Kuraokami]] and others | gender = Male }} '''Kagutsuchi''' (カグツチ; [[Old Japanese]]: ''Kagututi''), also known as '''Hi-no-Kagutsuchi''' or '''Homusubi''' among other names, is the [[kami]] of [[:en:Fire worship|fire]] in classical [[Japanese mythology]].{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Kagutsuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85|access-date=2020-12-16|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} ==Mythology== Kagutsuchi's birth burned his mother [[Izanami]], causing her death. His father [[Izanagi]], in his grief, beheaded Kagutsuchi with his sword, [[Ame-no-ohabari|Ame no Ohabari]] (天之尾羽張), and cut his body into eight pieces, which became eight [[volcano]]es. Kagutsuchi's corpse created numerous deities, which typically includes [[Watatsumi]], [[Kuraokami]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Futsunushi]], [[:en:Amatsumikaboshi|Amatsumikaboshi]], and [[Ōyamatsumi]].{{cite web |last1=Cartwright |first1=Mark |title=Kagutsuchi |url=https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |website=World History Encyclopedia |publisher=World History Publishing |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20210412182013/https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |archive-date=12 April 2021 |date=27 June 2017 |url-status=live}} {{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Ōyamatsumi|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9706|access-date=2024-07-05|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} Kagutsuchi's birth, in [[Japanese mythology]], comes at the end of the creation of the world and marks the beginning of death.{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} In the ''[[Engishiki]]'', a source which contains the myth, Izanami, in her death throes, bears the water goddess [[Mizuhanome]], instructing her to pacify Kagu-tsuchi if he should become violent. This story also contains references to traditional fire-fighting tools: gourds for carrying water and wet clay and water reeds for smothering fires. ==Name== The name ''Kagutsuchi'' was originally a compound phrase, consisting of ''kagu'', an Old Japanese root verb meaning "to shine"; ''tsu'', the Old Japanese possessive particle; and ''chi'', an Old Japanese root meaning "force, power".{{nihongo|Kokugo Dai Jiten, Revised Edition|国語大辞典（新装版）}} (in Japanese), [[Tōkyō]]: [[Shogakukan]], 1988 ==Popular culture== {{In popular culture|section|date=March 2023}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Sailor Moon]]'', a character named [[Hino Rei]] uses flame magic. {{Dubious|date=March 2023|reason=Wikipedia's pages about the various Hikawa shrines indicate that Susanoo-no-Mikoto is enshrined in them, not Kagu-tsuchi.}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Kimetsu no Yaiba]]'', there is a dance called Hinokami Kagura (lit. Dance of the Fire God), referencing Kagu-tsuchi, that is later transformed into a Breathing Style by the protagonist, [[Tanjiro Kamado]]. * In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Fairy Tail (anime)|Fairy Tail]]'', a character named [[List of Fairy Tail characters#Grimoire Heart|Zancrow]] uses the Flame God Slayer Magic and has an attack named after Kagutsuchi. * In the manga ''Noragami'', Kagutsuchi appears during the subjugation of Bishamon and is shown to be capable of blowing fire. * In the anime ''[[Mai-HiME]]'', Mai's Child is based upon Kagutsuchi. * In the manga and anime ''[[Naruto: Shippuden]]'', one of the main characters, [[Sasuke Uchiha]], is shown to have the ability to manipulate the black flames of Amaterasu into various weapons. This is referred to as Blaze Release: Kagutsuchi, which refers to the fire god. * In the anime ''[[:en:Ao No Exorcist|Ao No Exorcist]]'', the hometown to several of the characters is Kyoto, where a powerful sword was made by Kagu-Tsuchi long ago. * In the video game series ''[[:en:BlazBlue|BlazBlue]]'', the main setting of the first two games is the 13th Hierarchical City of Kagutsuchi. ** In the same video game series, one of the playable characters, Nine the Phantom, can summon Hi no Kagutsuchi, an enormous fire elemental. * In the video game series ''[[:en:Brave Frontier|Brave Frontier]]'', Kagatsuchi is a fire-based battle unit. He takes the form of a centaur, and is described as "a disastrous beast". * In the manga ''[[Rurouni Kenshin]]'', the main antagonist of the Kyoto Arc, [[Shishio Makoto]], uses a technique called "The Final Secret Sword: Kagutsuchi" as a last resort "trump card", bringing down a cyclone of flames that his sword can produce at his enemy. * In the ''[[Shin Megami Tensei]]'' and ''[[Persona (series)|Persona]]'' franchises, Kagutsuchi is a recurring figure in various titles. In some he is a regular demon to be recruited, a sword bearing his name in reference to the novels the franchises are based on, and in others he is a central figure to the plot. * In the video game ''[[:en:Call of Duty: Black Ops II|Call of Duty: Black Ops II]]'', a fire-elemental staff known as "Kagutsuchi's Blood" can be obtained on the Zombies map "Origins". * In the mobile phone game ''[[Puzzle and Dragons]]'', developed by Gung-ho, Hino Kagutsuchi is a collectible fire monster. * In the card game ''[[Yu-Gi-Oh!]]'', alongside ''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'', ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and ''[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]]'', Kagutsuchi is one of the 4 Xyz Monsters of Bujin. * In the mobile game ''[[Monster Strike]]'', Kagutsuchi is a fire collectible, along with [[Susano'o]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Inari Ōkami|Inari]], [[Izanami]], [[Izanagi]] and more. * In the mobile game ''[[:en:Dragalia Lost|Dragalia Lost]]'', Kagutsuchi appears as a red Flame-type dragon with golden armor fused to his body, given the title of "Incandescent General". He is described as the rival of the game's similarly-dragonized version of [[Marishi-Ten]], with the two of them inadvertently creating a magical hot spring in the Hogo region of Hinomoto, the game's version of Japan, during one of their duels, when Marishiten caused Kagutsuchi's molten blood to seep into the spring's water, permanently heating it and leading the locals to dub it the Bloodspring or Twin-Wyrm Spring in honor of the dragons' eventual friendship. When Kagutsuchi is not waging war, he is usually basking in a hot spring or frying chicken, which he offers to his allies. *In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Gintama|Gintama]]'', the main weapon aboard the Altana Liberation Army's flag ship Amenotorifune, intended to destroy the Earth, is called "Hinokagutsuchi". * In the video game ''[[:en:Ghost of Tsushima|Ghost of Tsushima]]'', there is a Charm of Kagu-Tsuchi. This charm is granted by finding and honoring the Plum Blossom Shrine. * In the light novel ''[[High School DxD]]: Slash/Dog'' series, Kagu-tsuchi is the Shinto God of Fire and also the patron deity of the Himejima Clan whom in turned blessed them with the power of spiritual flames. A descendant of the Himejima, Tobio Ikuse is the wielder of the Ame no Ohabari, the divine sword which killed Kagu-tsuchi. The sword is infused with Kagu-tsuchi's divine flames, which grant it immense power to kill even a Demon Lord Gressil. * In the video game series ''[[The Alchemist Code]]'', the royal family of Wadatsumi, Rising Ashes, which consists of Mitsuha, Kagura, Zeke Crowley and Logi Crowley, uses a fire ability called Kagutsuchi, which takes the form of a phoenix when used. This technique is very powerful against a type of enemy, ogres. In difficult situations, Mitsuha, Kagura and Logi use an advanced form of the fire technique, called "Kagutsuchi Kiraboshi". * In the 2022 video game ''[[:en:God of War Ragnarök|God of War Ragnarök]]'', it is implied that [[Týr]] reflects upon his knowledge of Kagu-Tsuchi as an inspiration for his combat tactics during his final encounter against [[Kratos (God of War)|Kratos]] in the game's free, and seemingly final downloadable content patch released in early December 2023. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Kōjin]] * [[Takemikazuchi]] * [[List of fire gods]] * [[Musubi]], part of the etymology of Homusubi ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== *{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} *Bock, Felicia G., trans. [https://archive.org/details/lccn_68-58914/page/n7/mode/2up.|''Engi-shiki: Procedures of the Engi Era'']. ASU Center for Asian Studies (Occasional Paper #17) ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85 Kagutsuchi], Encyclopedia of Shinto *Kagutsuchi on the [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kagutsuchi Japanese History Database]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Fire gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Killed deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Akiha faith]] }} [[sn:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[en:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[de:Kagutsuchi]] [[es:Kagutsuchi]] [[fa:کاگو-تسوچی]] [[fr:Kagutsuchi]] [[it:Kagutsuchi]] [[ja:カグツチ]] [[mni:ꯀꯒꯨ-ꯁꯨꯆꯤ]] [[pl:Kagutsuchi]] [[ru:Кагуцути]] [[sv:Kagutsuchi]] [[ta:ககுட்சூசி]] [[tr:Kagutsuchi]] [[uk:Кагу-цуті]] [[zh:迦具土]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]

This story also contains references to traditional fire-fighting tools: gourds for carrying water and wet clay and water reeds for smothering fires. ==Name== The name ''Kagutsuchi'' was originally a compound phrase, consisting of ''kagu'', an Old Japanese root verb meaning "to shine"; ''tsu'', the Old Japanese possessive particle; and ''chi'', an Old Japanese root meaning "force, power".{{nihongo|Kokugo Dai Jiten, Revised Edition|国語大辞典（新装版）}} (in Japanese), [[Tōkyō]]: [[Shogakukan]], 1988 ==Popular culture== {{In popular culture|section|date=March 2023}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Sailor Moon]]'', a character named [[Hino Rei]] uses flame magic. {{Dubious|date=March 2023|reason=Wikipedia's pages about the various Hikawa shrines indicate that Susanoo-no-Mikoto is enshrined in them, not Kagu-tsuchi.}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Kimetsu no Yaiba]]'', there is a dance called Hinokami Kagura (lit. Dance of the Fire God), referencing Kagu-tsuchi, that is later transformed into a Breathing Style by the protagonist, [[Tanjiro Kamado]]. * In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Fairy Tail (anime)|Fairy Tail]]'', a character named [[List of Fairy Tail characters#Grimoire Heart|Zancrow]] uses the Flame God Slayer Magic and has an attack named after Kagutsuchi. * In the manga ''Noragami'', Kagutsuchi appears during the subjugation of Bishamon and is shown to be capable of blowing fire. * In the anime ''[[Mai-HiME]]'', Mai's Child is based upon Kagutsuchi. * In the manga and anime ''[[Naruto: Shippuden]]'', one of the main characters, [[Sasuke Uchiha]], is shown to have the ability to manipulate the black flames of Amaterasu into various weapons. This is referred to as Blaze Release: Kagutsuchi, which refers to the fire god. * In the anime ''[[:en:Ao No Exorcist|Ao No Exorcist]]'', the hometown to several of the characters is Kyoto, where a powerful sword was made by Kagu-Tsuchi long ago. * In the video game series ''[[:en:BlazBlue|BlazBlue]]'', the main setting of the first two games is the 13th Hierarchical City of Kagutsuchi. ** In the same video game series, one of the playable characters, Nine the Phantom, can summon Hi no Kagutsuchi, an enormous fire elemental. * In the video game series ''[[:en:Brave Frontier|Brave Frontier]]'', Kagatsuchi is a fire-based battle unit. In some he is a regular demon to be recruited, a sword bearing his name in reference to the novels the franchises are based on, and in others he is a central figure to the plot. * In the video game ''[[:en:Call of Duty: Black Ops II|Call of Duty: Black Ops II]]'', a fire-elemental staff known as "Kagutsuchi's Blood" can be obtained on the Zombies map "Origins". * In the mobile phone game ''[[Puzzle and Dragons]]'', developed by Gung-ho, Hino Kagutsuchi is a collectible fire monster. * In the card game ''[[Yu-Gi-Oh!]]'', alongside ''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'', ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and ''[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]]'', Kagutsuchi is one of the 4 Xyz Monsters of Bujin. * In the mobile game ''[[Monster Strike]]'', Kagutsuchi is a fire collectible, along with [[Susano'o]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Inari Ōkami|Inari]], [[Izanami]], [[Izanagi]] and more. * In the mobile game ''[[:en:Dragalia Lost|Dragalia Lost]]'', Kagutsuchi appears as a red Flame-type dragon with golden armor fused to his body, given the title of "Incandescent General". He is described as the rival of the game's similarly-dragonized version of [[Marishi-Ten]], with the two of them inadvertently creating a magical hot spring in the Hogo region of Hinomoto, the game's version of Japan, during one of their duels, when Marishiten caused Kagutsuchi's molten blood to seep into the spring's water, permanently heating it and leading the locals to dub it the Bloodspring or Twin-Wyrm Spring in honor of the dragons' eventual friendship. A descendant of the Himejima, Tobio Ikuse is the wielder of the Ame no Ohabari, the divine sword which killed Kagu-tsuchi. The sword is infused with Kagu-tsuchi's divine flames, which grant it immense power to kill even a Demon Lord Gressil. * In the video game series ''[[The Alchemist Code]]'', the royal family of Wadatsumi, Rising Ashes, which consists of Mitsuha, Kagura, Zeke Crowley and Logi Crowley, uses a fire ability called Kagutsuchi, which takes the form of a phoenix when used. This technique is very powerful against a type of enemy, ogres. In difficult situations, Mitsuha, Kagura and Logi use an advanced form of the fire technique, called "Kagutsuchi Kiraboshi". * In the 2022 video game ''[[:en:God of War Ragnarök|God of War Ragnarök]]'', it is implied that [[Týr]] reflects upon his knowledge of Kagu-Tsuchi as an inspiration for his combat tactics during his final encounter against [[Kratos (God of War)|Kratos]] in the game's free, and seemingly final downloadable content patch released in early December 2023. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Kōjin]] * [[Takemikazuchi]] * [[List of fire gods]] * [[Musubi]], part of the etymology of Homusubi ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== *{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} *Bock, Felicia G., trans. Kagutsuchi's corpse created numerous deities, which typically includes [[Watatsumi]], [[Kuraokami]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Futsunushi]], [[:en:Amatsumikaboshi|Amatsumikaboshi]], and [[Ōyamatsumi]].{{cite web |last1=Cartwright |first1=Mark |title=Kagutsuchi |url=https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |website=World History Encyclopedia |publisher=World History Publishing |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20210412182013/https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |archive-date=12 April 2021 |date=27 June 2017 |url-status=live}} {{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Ōyamatsumi|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9706|access-date=2024-07-05|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} Kagutsuchi's birth, in [[Japanese mythology]], comes at the end of the creation of the world and marks the beginning of death.{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} In the ''[[Engishiki]]'', a source which contains the myth, Izanami, in her death throes, bears the water goddess [[Mizuhanome]], instructing her to pacify Kagu-tsuchi if he should become violent. This story also contains references to traditional fire-fighting tools: gourds for carrying water and wet clay and water reeds for smothering fires. ==Name== The name ''Kagutsuchi'' was originally a compound phrase, consisting of ''kagu'', an Old Japanese root verb meaning "to shine"; ''tsu'', the Old Japanese possessive particle; and ''chi'', an Old Japanese root meaning "force, power".{{nihongo|Kokugo Dai Jiten, Revised Edition|国語大辞典（新装版）}} (in Japanese), [[Tōkyō]]: [[Shogakukan]], 1988 ==Popular culture== {{In popular culture|section|date=March 2023}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Sailor Moon]]'', a character named [[Hino Rei]] uses flame magic. {{Dubious|date=March 2023|reason=Wikipedia's pages about the various Hikawa shrines indicate that Susanoo-no-Mikoto is enshrined in them, not Kagu-tsuchi.}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Kimetsu no Yaiba]]'', there is a dance called Hinokami Kagura (lit. Dance of the Fire God), referencing Kagu-tsuchi, that is later transformed into a Breathing Style by the protagonist, [[Tanjiro Kamado]]. * In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Fairy Tail (anime)|Fairy Tail]]'', a character named [[List of Fairy Tail characters#Grimoire Heart|Zancrow]] uses the Flame God Slayer Magic and has an attack named after Kagutsuchi. * In the manga ''Noragami'', Kagutsuchi appears during the subjugation of Bishamon and is shown to be capable of blowing fire. * In the anime ''[[Mai-HiME]]'', Mai's Child is based upon Kagutsuchi. * In the manga and anime ''[[Naruto: Shippuden]]'', one of the main characters, [[Sasuke Uchiha]], is shown to have the ability to manipulate the black flames of Amaterasu into various weapons. This is referred to as Blaze Release: Kagutsuchi, which refers to the fire god. * In the anime ''[[:en:Ao No Exorcist|Ao No Exorcist]]'', the hometown to several of the characters is Kyoto, where a powerful sword was made by Kagu-Tsuchi long ago. * In the video game series ''[[:en:BlazBlue|BlazBlue]]'', the main setting of the first two games is the 13th Hierarchical City of Kagutsuchi. ** In the same video game series, one of the playable characters, Nine the Phantom, can summon Hi no Kagutsuchi, an enormous fire elemental. * In the video game series ''[[:en:Brave Frontier|Brave Frontier]]'', Kagatsuchi is a fire-based battle unit. He takes the form of a centaur, and is described as "a disastrous beast". * In the manga ''[[Rurouni Kenshin]]'', the main antagonist of the Kyoto Arc, [[Shishio Makoto]], uses a technique called "The Final Secret Sword: Kagutsuchi" as a last resort "trump card", bringing down a cyclone of flames that his sword can produce at his enemy. * In the ''[[Shin Megami Tensei]]'' and ''[[Persona (series)|Persona]]'' franchises, Kagutsuchi is a recurring figure in various titles. In some he is a regular demon to be recruited, a sword bearing his name in reference to the novels the franchises are based on, and in others he is a central figure to the plot. * In the video game ''[[:en:Call of Duty: Black Ops II|Call of Duty: Black Ops II]]'', a fire-elemental staff known as "Kagutsuchi's Blood" can be obtained on the Zombies map "Origins". * In the mobile phone game ''[[Puzzle and Dragons]]'', developed by Gung-ho, Hino Kagutsuchi is a collectible fire monster. * In the card game ''[[Yu-Gi-Oh!]]'', alongside ''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'', ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and ''[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]]'', Kagutsuchi is one of the 4 Xyz Monsters of Bujin. * In the mobile game ''[[Monster Strike]]'', Kagutsuchi is a fire collectible, along with [[Susano'o]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Inari Ōkami|Inari]], [[Izanami]], [[Izanagi]] and more. * In the mobile game ''[[:en:Dragalia Lost|Dragalia Lost]]'', Kagutsuchi appears as a red Flame-type dragon with golden armor fused to his body, given the title of "Incandescent General". He is described as the rival of the game's similarly-dragonized version of [[Marishi-Ten]], with the two of them inadvertently creating a magical hot spring in the Hogo region of Hinomoto, the game's version of Japan, during one of their duels, when Marishiten caused Kagutsuchi's molten blood to seep into the spring's water, permanently heating it and leading the locals to dub it the Bloodspring or Twin-Wyrm Spring in honor of the dragons' eventual friendship. When Kagutsuchi is not waging war, he is usually basking in a hot spring or frying chicken, which he offers to his allies. *In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Gintama|Gintama]]'', the main weapon aboard the Altana Liberation Army's flag ship Amenotorifune, intended to destroy the Earth, is called "Hinokagutsuchi". * In the video game ''[[:en:Ghost of Tsushima|Ghost of Tsushima]]'', there is a Charm of Kagu-Tsuchi. This charm is granted by finding and honoring the Plum Blossom Shrine. * In the light novel ''[[:en:High School DxD|High School DxD]]: Slash/Dog'' series, Kagu-tsuchi is the Shinto God of Fire and also the patron deity of the Himejima Clan whom in turned blessed them with the power of spiritual flames. A descendant of the Himejima, Tobio Ikuse is the wielder of the Ame no Ohabari, the divine sword which killed Kagu-tsuchi. The sword is infused with Kagu-tsuchi's divine flames, which grant it immense power to kill even a Demon Lord Gressil. * In the video game series ''[[The Alchemist Code]]'', the royal family of Wadatsumi, Rising Ashes, which consists of Mitsuha, Kagura, Zeke Crowley and Logi Crowley, uses a fire ability called Kagutsuchi, which takes the form of a phoenix when used. This technique is very powerful against a type of enemy, ogres. In difficult situations, Mitsuha, Kagura and Logi use an advanced form of the fire technique, called "Kagutsuchi Kiraboshi". * In the 2022 video game ''[[:en:God of War Ragnarök|God of War Ragnarök]]'', it is implied that [[Týr]] reflects upon his knowledge of Kagu-Tsuchi as an inspiration for his combat tactics during his final encounter against [[Kratos (God of War)|Kratos]] in the game's free, and seemingly final downloadable content patch released in early December 2023. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Kōjin]] * [[Takemikazuchi]] * [[List of fire gods]] * [[Musubi]], part of the etymology of Homusubi ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== *{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} *Bock, Felicia G., trans. [https://archive.org/details/lccn_68-58914/page/n7/mode/2up.|''Engi-shiki: Procedures of the Engi Era'']. ASU Center for Asian Studies (Occasional Paper #17) ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85 Kagutsuchi], Encyclopedia of Shinto *Kagutsuchi on the [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kagutsuchi Japanese History Database]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Fire gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Killed deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Akiha faith]] }} [[sn:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[en:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[de:Kagutsuchi]] [[es:Kagutsuchi]] [[fa:کاگو-تسوچی]] [[fr:Kagutsuchi]] [[it:Kagutsuchi]] [[ja:カグツチ]] [[mni:ꯀꯒꯨ-ꯁꯨꯆꯤ]] [[pl:Kagutsuchi]] [[ru:Кагуцути]] [[sv:Kagutsuchi]] [[ta:ககுட்சூசி]] [[tr:Kagutsuchi]] [[uk:Кагу-цуті]] [[zh:迦具土]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]

| cult_center = [[:ja:火男火売神社|Honoo-Honome Shrine]], [[:ja:産田神社|Ubuta Shrine]], [[:en:Hananoiwaya Shrine|Hananoiwaya Shrine]], [[Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|Akiha Shrine]], and others | parents = [[Izanagi]] (father)
[[Izanami]] (mother) | offspring = [[Nesaku]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Kuraokami]] and others | gender = Male }} '''Kagutsuchi''' (カグツチ; [[Old Japanese]]: ''Kagututi''), also known as '''Hi-no-Kagutsuchi''' or '''Homusubi''' among other names, is the [[kami]] of [[:en:Fire worship|fire]] in classical [[Japanese mythology]].{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Kagutsuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85|access-date=2020-12-16|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} ==Mythology== Kagutsuchi's birth burned his mother [[Izanami]], causing her death. His father [[Izanagi]], in his grief, beheaded Kagutsuchi with his sword, [[Ame-no-ohabari|Ame no Ohabari]] (天之尾羽張), and cut his body into eight pieces, which became eight [[volcano]]es. Kagutsuchi's corpse created numerous deities, which typically includes [[Watatsumi]], [[Kuraokami]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Futsunushi]], [[:en:Amatsumikaboshi|Amatsumikaboshi]], and [[Ōyamatsumi]].{{cite web |last1=Cartwright |first1=Mark |title=Kagutsuchi |url=https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |website=World History Encyclopedia |publisher=World History Publishing |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20210412182013/https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |archive-date=12 April 2021 |date=27 June 2017 |url-status=live}} {{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Ōyamatsumi|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9706|access-date=2024-07-05|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} Kagutsuchi's birth, in [[Japanese mythology]], comes at the end of the creation of the world and marks the beginning of death.{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} In the ''[[Engishiki]]'', a source which contains the myth, Izanami, in her death throes, bears the water goddess [[Mizuhanome]], instructing her to pacify Kagu-tsuchi if he should become violent. This story also contains references to traditional fire-fighting tools: gourds for carrying water and wet clay and water reeds for smothering fires. ==Name== The name ''Kagutsuchi'' was originally a compound phrase, consisting of ''kagu'', an Old Japanese root verb meaning "to shine"; ''tsu'', the Old Japanese possessive particle; and ''chi'', an Old Japanese root meaning "force, power".{{nihongo|Kokugo Dai Jiten, Revised Edition|国語大辞典（新装版）}} (in Japanese), [[Tōkyō]]: [[Shogakukan]], 1988 ==Popular culture== {{In popular culture|section|date=March 2023}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Sailor Moon]]'', a character named [[:en:Hino Rei|Hino Rei]] uses flame magic. {{Dubious|date=March 2023|reason=Wikipedia's pages about the various Hikawa shrines indicate that Susanoo-no-Mikoto is enshrined in them, not Kagu-tsuchi.}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Kimetsu no Yaiba]]'', there is a dance called Hinokami Kagura (lit. Dance of the Fire God), referencing Kagu-tsuchi, that is later transformed into a Breathing Style by the protagonist, [[Tanjiro Kamado]]. * In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Fairy Tail (anime)|Fairy Tail]]'', a character named [[List of Fairy Tail characters#Grimoire Heart|Zancrow]] uses the Flame God Slayer Magic and has an attack named after Kagutsuchi. * In the manga ''Noragami'', Kagutsuchi appears during the subjugation of Bishamon and is shown to be capable of blowing fire. * In the anime ''[[Mai-HiME]]'', Mai's Child is based upon Kagutsuchi. * In the manga and anime ''[[Naruto: Shippuden]]'', one of the main characters, [[Sasuke Uchiha]], is shown to have the ability to manipulate the black flames of Amaterasu into various weapons. This is referred to as Blaze Release: Kagutsuchi, which refers to the fire god. * In the anime ''[[:en:Ao No Exorcist|Ao No Exorcist]]'', the hometown to several of the characters is Kyoto, where a powerful sword was made by Kagu-Tsuchi long ago. * In the video game series ''[[:en:BlazBlue|BlazBlue]]'', the main setting of the first two games is the 13th Hierarchical City of Kagutsuchi. ** In the same video game series, one of the playable characters, Nine the Phantom, can summon Hi no Kagutsuchi, an enormous fire elemental. * In the video game series ''[[:en:Brave Frontier|Brave Frontier]]'', Kagatsuchi is a fire-based battle unit. He takes the form of a centaur, and is described as "a disastrous beast". * In the manga ''[[Rurouni Kenshin]]'', the main antagonist of the Kyoto Arc, [[Shishio Makoto]], uses a technique called "The Final Secret Sword: Kagutsuchi" as a last resort "trump card", bringing down a cyclone of flames that his sword can produce at his enemy. * In the ''[[Shin Megami Tensei]]'' and ''[[Persona (series)|Persona]]'' franchises, Kagutsuchi is a recurring figure in various titles. In some he is a regular demon to be recruited, a sword bearing his name in reference to the novels the franchises are based on, and in others he is a central figure to the plot. * In the video game ''[[:en:Call of Duty: Black Ops II|Call of Duty: Black Ops II]]'', a fire-elemental staff known as "Kagutsuchi's Blood" can be obtained on the Zombies map "Origins". * In the mobile phone game ''[[Puzzle and Dragons]]'', developed by Gung-ho, Hino Kagutsuchi is a collectible fire monster. * In the card game ''[[Yu-Gi-Oh!]]'', alongside ''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'', ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and ''[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]]'', Kagutsuchi is one of the 4 Xyz Monsters of Bujin. * In the mobile game ''[[Monster Strike]]'', Kagutsuchi is a fire collectible, along with [[Susano'o]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Inari Ōkami|Inari]], [[Izanami]], [[Izanagi]] and more. * In the mobile game ''[[:en:Dragalia Lost|Dragalia Lost]]'', Kagutsuchi appears as a red Flame-type dragon with golden armor fused to his body, given the title of "Incandescent General". He is described as the rival of the game's similarly-dragonized version of [[Marishi-Ten]], with the two of them inadvertently creating a magical hot spring in the Hogo region of Hinomoto, the game's version of Japan, during one of their duels, when Marishiten caused Kagutsuchi's molten blood to seep into the spring's water, permanently heating it and leading the locals to dub it the Bloodspring or Twin-Wyrm Spring in honor of the dragons' eventual friendship. When Kagutsuchi is not waging war, he is usually basking in a hot spring or frying chicken, which he offers to his allies. *In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Gintama|Gintama]]'', the main weapon aboard the Altana Liberation Army's flag ship Amenotorifune, intended to destroy the Earth, is called "Hinokagutsuchi". * In the video game ''[[:en:Ghost of Tsushima|Ghost of Tsushima]]'', there is a Charm of Kagu-Tsuchi. This charm is granted by finding and honoring the Plum Blossom Shrine. * In the light novel ''[[:en:High School DxD|High School DxD]]: Slash/Dog'' series, Kagu-tsuchi is the Shinto God of Fire and also the patron deity of the Himejima Clan whom in turned blessed them with the power of spiritual flames. A descendant of the Himejima, Tobio Ikuse is the wielder of the Ame no Ohabari, the divine sword which killed Kagu-tsuchi. The sword is infused with Kagu-tsuchi's divine flames, which grant it immense power to kill even a Demon Lord Gressil. * In the video game series ''[[The Alchemist Code]]'', the royal family of Wadatsumi, Rising Ashes, which consists of Mitsuha, Kagura, Zeke Crowley and Logi Crowley, uses a fire ability called Kagutsuchi, which takes the form of a phoenix when used. This technique is very powerful against a type of enemy, ogres. In difficult situations, Mitsuha, Kagura and Logi use an advanced form of the fire technique, called "Kagutsuchi Kiraboshi". * In the 2022 video game ''[[:en:God of War Ragnarök|God of War Ragnarök]]'', it is implied that [[Týr]] reflects upon his knowledge of Kagu-Tsuchi as an inspiration for his combat tactics during his final encounter against [[Kratos (God of War)|Kratos]] in the game's free, and seemingly final downloadable content patch released in early December 2023. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Kōjin]] * [[Takemikazuchi]] * [[List of fire gods]] * [[Musubi]], part of the etymology of Homusubi ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== *{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} *Bock, Felicia G., trans. [https://archive.org/details/lccn_68-58914/page/n7/mode/2up.|''Engi-shiki: Procedures of the Engi Era'']. ASU Center for Asian Studies (Occasional Paper #17) ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85 Kagutsuchi], Encyclopedia of Shinto *Kagutsuchi on the [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kagutsuchi Japanese History Database]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Fire gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Killed deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Akiha faith]] }} [[sn:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[en:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[de:Kagutsuchi]] [[es:Kagutsuchi]] [[fa:کاگو-تسوچی]] [[fr:Kagutsuchi]] [[it:Kagutsuchi]] [[ja:カグツチ]] [[mni:ꯀꯒꯨ-ꯁꯨꯆꯤ]] [[pl:Kagutsuchi]] [[ru:Кагуцути]] [[sv:Kagutsuchi]] [[ta:ககுட்சூசி]] [[tr:Kagutsuchi]] [[uk:Кагу-цуті]] [[zh:迦具土]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]

Dance of the Fire God), referencing Kagu-tsuchi, that is later transformed into a Breathing Style by the protagonist, [[Tanjiro Kamado]]. * In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Fairy Tail (anime)|Fairy Tail]]'', a character named [[List of Fairy Tail characters#Grimoire Heart|Zancrow]] uses the Flame God Slayer Magic and has an attack named after Kagutsuchi. * In the manga ''Noragami'', Kagutsuchi appears during the subjugation of Bishamon and is shown to be capable of blowing fire. * In the anime ''[[Mai-HiME]]'', Mai's Child is based upon Kagutsuchi. * In the manga and anime ''[[Naruto: Shippuden]]'', one of the main characters, [[Sasuke Uchiha]], is shown to have the ability to manipulate the black flames of Amaterasu into various weapons. This is referred to as Blaze Release: Kagutsuchi, which refers to the fire god. * In the anime ''[[:en:Ao No Exorcist|Ao No Exorcist]]'', the hometown to several of the characters is Kyoto, where a powerful sword was made by Kagu-Tsuchi long ago. * In the video game series ''[[:en:BlazBlue|BlazBlue]]'', the main setting of the first two games is the 13th Hierarchical City of Kagutsuchi. ** In the same video game series, one of the playable characters, Nine the Phantom, can summon Hi no Kagutsuchi, an enormous fire elemental. * In the video game series ''[[:en:Brave Frontier|Brave Frontier]]'', Kagatsuchi is a fire-based battle unit. He takes the form of a centaur, and is described as "a disastrous beast". * In the manga ''[[Rurouni Kenshin]]'', the main antagonist of the Kyoto Arc, [[Shishio Makoto]], uses a technique called "The Final Secret Sword: Kagutsuchi" as a last resort "trump card", bringing down a cyclone of flames that his sword can produce at his enemy. * In the ''[[Shin Megami Tensei]]'' and ''[[Persona (series)|Persona]]'' franchises, Kagutsuchi is a recurring figure in various titles. In some he is a regular demon to be recruited, a sword bearing his name in reference to the novels the franchises are based on, and in others he is a central figure to the plot. * In the video game ''[[:en:Call of Duty: Black Ops II|Call of Duty: Black Ops II]]'', a fire-elemental staff known as "Kagutsuchi's Blood" can be obtained on the Zombies map "Origins". * In the mobile phone game ''[[Puzzle and Dragons]]'', developed by Gung-ho, Hino Kagutsuchi is a collectible fire monster. * In the card game ''[[Yu-Gi-Oh!]]'', alongside ''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'', ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and ''[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]]'', Kagutsuchi is one of the 4 Xyz Monsters of Bujin. * In the mobile game ''[[Monster Strike]]'', Kagutsuchi is a fire collectible, along with [[Susano'o]], [[Amaterasu]], [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari]], [[Izanami]], [[Izanagi]] and more. * In the mobile game ''[[:en:Dragalia Lost|Dragalia Lost]]'', Kagutsuchi appears as a red Flame-type dragon with golden armor fused to his body, given the title of "Incandescent General". He is described as the rival of the game's similarly-dragonized version of [[Marishi-Ten]], with the two of them inadvertently creating a magical hot spring in the Hogo region of Hinomoto, the game's version of Japan, during one of their duels, when Marishiten caused Kagutsuchi's molten blood to seep into the spring's water, permanently heating it and leading the locals to dub it the Bloodspring or Twin-Wyrm Spring in honor of the dragons' eventual friendship. When Kagutsuchi is not waging war, he is usually basking in a hot spring or frying chicken, which he offers to his allies. *In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Gintama|Gintama]]'', the main weapon aboard the Altana Liberation Army's flag ship Amenotorifune, intended to destroy the Earth, is called "Hinokagutsuchi". * In the video game ''[[:en:Ghost of Tsushima|Ghost of Tsushima]]'', there is a Charm of Kagu-Tsuchi. This charm is granted by finding and honoring the Plum Blossom Shrine. * In the light novel ''[[:en:High School DxD|High School DxD]]: Slash/Dog'' series, Kagu-tsuchi is the Shinto God of Fire and also the patron deity of the Himejima Clan whom in turned blessed them with the power of spiritual flames. A descendant of the Himejima, Tobio Ikuse is the wielder of the Ame no Ohabari, the divine sword which killed Kagu-tsuchi. The sword is infused with Kagu-tsuchi's divine flames, which grant it immense power to kill even a Demon Lord Gressil. * In the video game series ''[[The Alchemist Code]]'', the royal family of Wadatsumi, Rising Ashes, which consists of Mitsuha, Kagura, Zeke Crowley and Logi Crowley, uses a fire ability called Kagutsuchi, which takes the form of a phoenix when used. This technique is very powerful against a type of enemy, ogres. In difficult situations, Mitsuha, Kagura and Logi use an advanced form of the fire technique, called "Kagutsuchi Kiraboshi". * In the 2022 video game ''[[:en:God of War Ragnarök|God of War Ragnarök]]'', it is implied that [[Týr]] reflects upon his knowledge of Kagu-Tsuchi as an inspiration for his combat tactics during his final encounter against [[Kratos (God of War)|Kratos]] in the game's free, and seemingly final downloadable content patch released in early December 2023. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Kōjin]] * [[Takemikazuchi]] * [[List of fire gods]] * [[Musubi]], part of the etymology of Homusubi ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== *{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} *Bock, Felicia G., trans. [https://archive.org/details/lccn_68-58914/page/n7/mode/2up.|''Engi-shiki: Procedures of the Engi Era'']. ASU Center for Asian Studies (Occasional Paper #17) ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85 Kagutsuchi], Encyclopedia of Shinto *Kagutsuchi on the [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kagutsuchi Japanese History Database]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Fire gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Killed deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Akiha faith]] }} [[sn:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[en:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[de:Kagutsuchi]] [[es:Kagutsuchi]] [[fa:کاگو-تسوچی]] [[fr:Kagutsuchi]] [[it:Kagutsuchi]] [[ja:カグツチ]] [[mni:ꯀꯒꯨ-ꯁꯨꯆꯤ]] [[pl:Kagutsuchi]] [[ru:Кагуцути]] [[sv:Kagutsuchi]] [[ta:ககுட்சூசி]] [[tr:Kagutsuchi]] [[uk:Кагу-цуті]] [[zh:迦具土]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]

{{Short description|Japanese fire god}} {{multiple issues|{{morefootnotes|date=January 2016}} {{refimprove|date=January 2016}}}} {{Infobox deity | type = Japanese | name = Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami | deity_of = ''[[Kami]]'' of [[List of fire gods|fire]] and the [[:en:Hearth|Hearth]] | script_name = Japanese | script = 火之迦具土神 | other_names = Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto (軻遇突智命, 加具土命)
Homusubi (火産霊)
Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami (火之夜藝速男神)
Hi-no-Yakihayao-no-Mikoto-no-Kami (火焼速男命神)
Hi-no-Yakihiko-no-Kami (火焼彦神)
Ho-no-Yakezumi-no-Kami (火焼炭神)
| cult_center = [[:ja:火男火売神社|Honoo-Honome Shrine]], [[:ja:産田神社|Ubuta Shrine]], [[:en:Hananoiwaya Shrine|Hananoiwaya Shrine]], [[Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|Akiha Shrine]], and others | parents = [[Izanagi]] (father)
[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] (mother) | offspring = [[Nesaku]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Kuraokami]] and others | gender = Male }} '''Kagutsuchi''' (カグツチ; [[Old Japanese]]: ''Kagututi''), also known as '''Hi-no-Kagutsuchi''' or '''Homusubi''' among other names, is the [[kami]] of [[:en:Fire worship|fire]] in classical [[Japanese mythology]].{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Kagutsuchi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85|access-date=2020-12-16|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} ==Mythology== Kagutsuchi's birth burned his mother [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], causing her death. His father [[Izanagi]], in his grief, beheaded Kagutsuchi with his sword, [[Ame-no-ohabari|Ame no Ohabari]] (天之尾羽張), and cut his body into eight pieces, which became eight [[volcano]]es. Kagutsuchi's corpse created numerous deities, which typically includes [[Watatsumi]], [[Kuraokami]], [[Takemikazuchi]], [[Futsunushi]], [[:en:Amatsumikaboshi|Amatsumikaboshi]], and [[Ōyamatsumi]].{{cite web |last1=Cartwright |first1=Mark |title=Kagutsuchi |url=https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |website=World History Encyclopedia |publisher=World History Publishing |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20210412182013/https://www.worldhistory.org/Kagutsuchi/ |archive-date=12 April 2021 |date=27 June 2017 |url-status=live}} {{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Ōyamatsumi|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9706|access-date=2024-07-05|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} Kagutsuchi's birth, in [[Japanese mythology]], comes at the end of the creation of the world and marks the beginning of death.{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} In the ''[[Engishiki]]'', a source which contains the myth, Izanami, in her death throes, bears the water goddess [[Mizuhanome]], instructing her to pacify Kagu-tsuchi if he should become violent. This story also contains references to traditional fire-fighting tools: gourds for carrying water and wet clay and water reeds for smothering fires. ==Name== The name ''Kagutsuchi'' was originally a compound phrase, consisting of ''kagu'', an Old Japanese root verb meaning "to shine"; ''tsu'', the Old Japanese possessive particle; and ''chi'', an Old Japanese root meaning "force, power".{{nihongo|Kokugo Dai Jiten, Revised Edition|国語大辞典（新装版）}} (in Japanese), [[Tōkyō]]: [[Shogakukan]], 1988 ==Popular culture== {{In popular culture|section|date=March 2023}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Sailor Moon]]'', a character named [[:en:Hino Rei|Hino Rei]] uses flame magic. {{Dubious|date=March 2023|reason=Wikipedia's pages about the various Hikawa shrines indicate that Susanoo-no-Mikoto is enshrined in them, not Kagu-tsuchi.}} * In the manga and anime ''[[Kimetsu no Yaiba]]'', there is a dance called Hinokami Kagura (lit. Dance of the Fire God), referencing Kagu-tsuchi, that is later transformed into a Breathing Style by the protagonist, [[Tanjiro Kamado]]. * In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Fairy Tail (anime)|Fairy Tail]]'', a character named [[List of Fairy Tail characters#Grimoire Heart|Zancrow]] uses the Flame God Slayer Magic and has an attack named after Kagutsuchi. * In the manga ''Noragami'', Kagutsuchi appears during the subjugation of Bishamon and is shown to be capable of blowing fire. * In the anime ''[[Mai-HiME]]'', Mai's Child is based upon Kagutsuchi. * In the manga and anime ''[[Naruto: Shippuden]]'', one of the main characters, [[Sasuke Uchiha]], is shown to have the ability to manipulate the black flames of Amaterasu into various weapons. This is referred to as Blaze Release: Kagutsuchi, which refers to the fire god. * In the anime ''[[:en:Ao No Exorcist|Ao No Exorcist]]'', the hometown to several of the characters is Kyoto, where a powerful sword was made by Kagu-Tsuchi long ago. * In the video game series ''[[:en:BlazBlue|BlazBlue]]'', the main setting of the first two games is the 13th Hierarchical City of Kagutsuchi. ** In the same video game series, one of the playable characters, Nine the Phantom, can summon Hi no Kagutsuchi, an enormous fire elemental. * In the video game series ''[[:en:Brave Frontier|Brave Frontier]]'', Kagatsuchi is a fire-based battle unit. He takes the form of a centaur, and is described as "a disastrous beast". * In the manga ''[[Rurouni Kenshin]]'', the main antagonist of the Kyoto Arc, [[Shishio Makoto]], uses a technique called "The Final Secret Sword: Kagutsuchi" as a last resort "trump card", bringing down a cyclone of flames that his sword can produce at his enemy. * In the ''[[Shin Megami Tensei]]'' and ''[[Persona (series)|Persona]]'' franchises, Kagutsuchi is a recurring figure in various titles. In some he is a regular demon to be recruited, a sword bearing his name in reference to the novels the franchises are based on, and in others he is a central figure to the plot. * In the video game ''[[:en:Call of Duty: Black Ops II|Call of Duty: Black Ops II]]'', a fire-elemental staff known as "Kagutsuchi's Blood" can be obtained on the Zombies map "Origins". * In the mobile phone game ''[[Puzzle and Dragons]]'', developed by Gung-ho, Hino Kagutsuchi is a collectible fire monster. * In the card game ''[[Yu-Gi-Oh!]]'', alongside ''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]'', ''[[Amaterasu]]'', and ''[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]]'', Kagutsuchi is one of the 4 Xyz Monsters of Bujin. * In the mobile game ''[[Monster Strike]]'', Kagutsuchi is a fire collectible, along with [[Susano'o]], [[Amaterasu]], [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], [[Izanagi]] and more. * In the mobile game ''[[:en:Dragalia Lost|Dragalia Lost]]'', Kagutsuchi appears as a red Flame-type dragon with golden armor fused to his body, given the title of "Incandescent General". He is described as the rival of the game's similarly-dragonized version of [[Marishi-Ten]], with the two of them inadvertently creating a magical hot spring in the Hogo region of Hinomoto, the game's version of Japan, during one of their duels, when Marishiten caused Kagutsuchi's molten blood to seep into the spring's water, permanently heating it and leading the locals to dub it the Bloodspring or Twin-Wyrm Spring in honor of the dragons' eventual friendship. When Kagutsuchi is not waging war, he is usually basking in a hot spring or frying chicken, which he offers to his allies. *In the manga and anime ''[[:en:Gintama|Gintama]]'', the main weapon aboard the Altana Liberation Army's flag ship Amenotorifune, intended to destroy the Earth, is called "Hinokagutsuchi". * In the video game ''[[:en:Ghost of Tsushima|Ghost of Tsushima]]'', there is a Charm of Kagu-Tsuchi. This charm is granted by finding and honoring the Plum Blossom Shrine. * In the light novel ''[[:en:High School DxD|High School DxD]]: Slash/Dog'' series, Kagu-tsuchi is the Shinto God of Fire and also the patron deity of the Himejima Clan whom in turned blessed them with the power of spiritual flames. A descendant of the Himejima, Tobio Ikuse is the wielder of the Ame no Ohabari, the divine sword which killed Kagu-tsuchi. The sword is infused with Kagu-tsuchi's divine flames, which grant it immense power to kill even a Demon Lord Gressil. * In the video game series ''[[The Alchemist Code]]'', the royal family of Wadatsumi, Rising Ashes, which consists of Mitsuha, Kagura, Zeke Crowley and Logi Crowley, uses a fire ability called Kagutsuchi, which takes the form of a phoenix when used. This technique is very powerful against a type of enemy, ogres. In difficult situations, Mitsuha, Kagura and Logi use an advanced form of the fire technique, called "Kagutsuchi Kiraboshi". * In the 2022 video game ''[[:en:God of War Ragnarök|God of War Ragnarök]]'', it is implied that [[Týr]] reflects upon his knowledge of Kagu-Tsuchi as an inspiration for his combat tactics during his final encounter against [[Kratos (God of War)|Kratos]] in the game's free, and seemingly final downloadable content patch released in early December 2023. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo}} == See also == * [[Kōjin]] * [[Takemikazuchi]] * [[List of fire gods]] * [[Musubi]], part of the etymology of Homusubi ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== *{{cite book |last1=Ashkenazi |first1=Michael |title=Handbook of Japanese Mythology |date=5 November 2003 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-57607-468-8 |page=186 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Tb3OEAAAQBAJ&q=%22the%20start%20of%20death%22 |language=en}} *Bock, Felicia G., trans. [https://archive.org/details/lccn_68-58914/page/n7/mode/2up.|''Engi-shiki: Procedures of the Engi Era'']. ASU Center for Asian Studies (Occasional Paper #17) ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=85 Kagutsuchi], Encyclopedia of Shinto *Kagutsuchi on the [https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kagutsuchi Japanese History Database]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Fire gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Killed deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Akiha faith]] }} [[sn:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[en:Kagu-tsuchi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[de:Kagutsuchi]] [[es:Kagutsuchi]] [[fa:کاگو-تسوچی]] [[fr:Kagutsuchi]] [[it:Kagutsuchi]] [[ja:カグツチ]] [[mni:ꯀꯒꯨ-ꯁꯨꯆꯤ]] [[pl:Kagutsuchi]] [[ru:Кагуцути]] [[sv:Kagutsuchi]] [[ta:ககுட்சூசி]] [[tr:Kagutsuchi]] [[uk:Кагу-цуті]] [[zh:迦具土]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]
